thesis: Strongest cross-sectional momentum in the universe with rising analyst revisions and a fair valuation — a specialty-chemicals/healthcare CDMO ride where two independent forces agree, so I lead the book with it.
falsification: Price momentum rolls over or revisions turn down over the next 1-3 months, or it lags the benchmark while lower-momentum peers rally.

thesis: Injectables/CDMO turnaround with the strongest rising-revision profile in the universe and constructive momentum at a fair valuation — the highest-conviction structural hold.
falsification: Revisions stop rising or turn down, or USFDA/injectables newsflow forces estimate cuts.

thesis: Complex-generics/research small-cap with strong price momentum and rising analyst revisions — an early-cycle compounder where the revision tilt supports the trend.
falsification: Momentum fades or revisions flatten; small-cap liquidity forces the guardrail to clip it hard with no follow-through.

thesis: API-to-formulations scale story with strong momentum and rising revisions — a cyclical with operating leverage as US generics pricing stabilizes.
falsification: Momentum or revisions roll over, or pricing pressure in core molecules reverses the earnings trend.

thesis: Para-IV/complex-generics optionality that is both cheap relative to peers and showing strong momentum with rising revisions — the best risk/reward where value and momentum agree.
falsification: A key US launch/litigation outcome disappoints, momentum breaks, or revisions turn down.

thesis: ARV-to-CDMO pivot with strong momentum and rising revisions as the non-ARV/contract pipeline ramps — a structural margin-mix improvement story.
falsification: CDMO ramp slips, revisions stall, or momentum reverses on a weak quarter.

thesis: Diversified US-generics-plus-specialty franchise with rising revisions and steady momentum — a liquid quality name that anchors the high-conviction sleeve.
falsification: US pipeline disappointments cut estimates, or momentum and revisions both soften.

thesis: OTC/generics niche exporter with strongly rising revisions and decent momentum — a small-cap with consistent execution where the revision tilt leads.
falsification: Revisions flatten, momentum turns negative, or currency/regulatory shock hits the export model.

thesis: Branded-generics and API franchise with clearly rising revisions; I hold it for the revision tailwind even though price momentum is only neutral.
falsification: Revisions turn down, or the neutral momentum decays into a downtrend over the next quarter.

thesis: Branded-domestic and CMO compounder with good momentum and steady revisions — a defensible quality hold in the core.
falsification: Momentum stalls and revisions slip negative, or domestic-brand growth decelerates.

thesis: CRDMO franchise with rising revisions and constructive momentum — a held name where the revision tilt still supports the position.
falsification: Revisions fade or momentum reverses; CRDMO order-book newsflow disappoints.

thesis: Respiratory/derma franchise with innovation optionality (Ichnos) where momentum is positive and revisions are mildly rising — a cyclical re-rating candidate.
falsification: Momentum reverses, revisions turn down, or pipeline/innovation newsflow disappoints.

thesis: Hospital operator with rising revisions after its India-GCC restructuring — a services-sleeve add where the revision tilt is constructive.
falsification: Revisions turn down, occupancy/ARPOB growth stalls, or momentum fails to follow the revision improvement.

thesis: LatAm/EM-focused generics compounder with rising revisions and steady execution — a quality small-cap add despite only neutral price momentum.
falsification: Revisions flatten or EM/regulatory/currency shock breaks the steady-growth model.

thesis: Single-specialty-to-multi diagnostics expansion with positive momentum and mildly rising revisions — anchors the diagnostics sleeve.
falsification: Revisions slip, momentum reverses, or new-center ramp disappoints on margins.

thesis: Asset-light diagnostics platform with rising revisions; I hold for the revision tailwind despite weak near-term price momentum.
falsification: Revisions turn down, or weak momentum persists into an outright downtrend.

thesis: Diagnostics chain with mildly rising revisions and stabilizing momentum — a diversifying services hold.
falsification: Revisions fade, competitive pricing pressure resumes, or momentum breaks down.

thesis: Highest-quality diagnostics franchise with rising revisions; held for revision support and defensive quality.
falsification: Revisions turn down or momentum deteriorates further without stabilizing.

thesis: Premier hospital-plus-pharmacy-plus-digital platform with positive momentum — the large-cap quality anchor that lowers book beta while staying in-trend.
falsification: Momentum reverses, or hospital/Apollo-24x7 unit economics disappoint and force estimate cuts.

thesis: Large diversified US-generics and biosimilars franchise that is reasonably cheap with mildly positive momentum and revisions — a liquid value-tilted core hold.
falsification: US pricing pressure resumes, revisions turn negative, or momentum breaks.

thesis: Branded-generics and ophthalmology franchise with decent momentum but softening revisions — I keep a reduced position rather than press it.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate strongly (I under-owned) or momentum collapses (I over-owned).

thesis: Large US-plus-domestic franchise with respiratory/complex-generics pipeline, mildly positive revisions and reasonable valuation — a liquid core hold.
falsification: Revisions turn down, a key US launch slips, or momentum decays into a downtrend.

thesis: High-quality domestic-branded compounder, but revisions are falling and it is expensive, so I cut the position even though price momentum is still positive.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate and momentum keeps leading — that would say I trimmed quality too early.

thesis: Anti-infectives leader with rising revisions; held for the revision tailwind despite soft momentum.
falsification: Revisions turn down or weak momentum deepens into a clear downtrend.

thesis: Largest, most liquid franchise with a growing specialty (derm/onco) book; neutral momentum and revisions but I hold it as a low-beta large-cap anchor.
falsification: Specialty franchise disappoints, revisions turn down, or it materially lags the sector.

thesis: Respiratory and US-inhalation franchise at a reasonable valuation — a liquid quality anchor despite soft momentum.
falsification: US inhalation pipeline slips, revisions turn down, or momentum deteriorates further.

thesis: Large US-generics franchise but with falling revisions and no momentum edge; I keep only a reduced liquid position pending a revision turn.
falsification: Revisions turn up and momentum re-accelerates — I would have under-owned a cheap large-cap.

thesis: Domestic-consumer and chronic franchise with flat momentum and falling revisions; I trim to a small position rather than back it here.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate on chronic/consumer momentum and the name leads.

thesis: Single-cluster hospital operator with rising revisions; held for the revision tailwind even though near-term momentum is weak.
falsification: Revisions fade or weak momentum turns into a sustained decline.

thesis: Hospital recovery story but with falling revisions and weak momentum; I cut it to a token position rather than exit fully given franchise quality.
falsification: Revisions and momentum both turn up — a recovery I under-owned.

thesis: Defensive MNC pharma with no momentum and flat revisions — dead weight in a momentum-led book, cut to a token weight.
falsification: A revision/momentum turn in the MNC-pharma cluster that I faded.

thesis: Defensive MNC vaccines/specialty name with weak momentum and soft revisions — reduced as low-momentum dead weight.
falsification: Momentum and revisions in the MNC cluster turn up against my fade.

thesis: Defensive consumer-health franchise with weak momentum and flat revisions — trimmed to a token weight in a momentum-led book.
falsification: A clear revision/momentum upturn in defensive consumer-health that I faded.

thesis: CDMO small-cap with strong momentum but revisions that have rolled over; I keep a reduced position rather than the full weight, respecting the deteriorating revision force.
falsification: Revisions re-accelerate and the CDMO order book strengthens — momentum without a revision break would have justified holding more.
